rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ALLIED IRISH BANKS PLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Further Proceeding On Recognition Of Foreign Country Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 13, 2018, Line 11, Further Proceeding On Recognition Of Foreign Country Judgment Hearing required. At the hearing the court will explain why the November 30, 2018 order needs to be vacated, this case will remain as a petition rather than a civil action per the court's internal procedures, and how the parties can still achieve their apparent objective of filing pre-hearing motions and, if needed, present live testimony at the hearing on the petition. In the nature of a case management conference, the court will also discuss setting future dates for management of this case. Counsel for Allied Irish Bank is required to ensure that counsel for Oisin Heneghan is aware of this hearing. If the date of the hearing is not convenient to counsel for both sides, counsel should meet and confer and notify the court by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org of at least two dates that are agreeable to counsel for both sides. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CDC SAN FRANCISCO LLC VS. WEBCOR CONSTRUCTION, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 13, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT OBAYASHI's CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Obayashi Corporation's demurrer to all three causes of action in the third amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. Because Obayashi's liability is either derivative of or based on the same allegations as the claims alleged against the Webcor defendants, the order sustaining the Webcor defendants' demurrer to all three causes of action in the third amended complaint without leave to amend requires that Obayahsi's demurrer also be sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action in the third amended complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Obayashi is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CDC SAN FRANCISCO LLC VS. WEBCOR CONSTRUCTION, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 13, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTs WEBCOR CONSTRUCTION, L.P., and WEBCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC. DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants Webcor Construction, L.P. and Webcor Construction, Inc.'s demurrer to all three causes of action in the third amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. Plaintiff CDC San Francisco LLC has not alleged, nor does it appear that it is able to allege, ultimate facts showing that any of its claims are not time-barred. The August 24, 2018 Order limited leave to amend to two areas and required specific allegations for both areas. The allegations in the third amended complaint fall well short of what was required to successfully avoid the bar of the statute of limitations for each of CDC's claims. CDC is unable to allege a viable equitable estoppel argument based on misrepresentation of the identity of the licensed entity performing work on the hotel project. Interrogatories and pleadings from the construction defect litigation cannot be used to toll the one-year statute of limitations because these statements occurred in 2017, well after the statute of limitations ran in March 2010. Kleinecke v. Montecito Water District (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 240 is distinguishable because in that case the defendant's tolling act occurred prior to expiration of the statute of limitations. Documents prepared and provided by third-party subcontractors and others referring to the name Webcor Builders, Inc. cannot provide a basis for estoppel because these alleged misrepresentations were not made by a Webcor entity. Most significantly, the judicially noticeable Notice of Completion of February 11, 2009 alone defeats CDC's argument that it did not become aware of the true legal name "Webcor Construction L.P. dba Webcor Builders" until 2017. Nor has CDC adequately alleged that any Webcor entity did unlicensed work on the hotel project within one year of the filing of this lawsuit. CDC's argument that bidding without the requisite license entitles CDC to disgorgement fails per the reasoning of MW Erectors, Inc. v. Niederhauser Ornamental & Metal Works Co., Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 412, 435-36. MW Erectors held that disgorgement under section 7031 does not apply where an entity enters into a contract before obtaining a license if that entity is licensed at all times it performs actual work on the contract. The bidding activities described in CDC's third amended complaint necessarily occurred pre-contract and do not constitute the performance of work under a contract that triggers section 7031 liability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Webcor defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: UNIONN LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. ROBERT H ELMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiff Unionn Llcs Person(S) Most Qualified, Defendant Nina Hatvany And Defendant Pacific Union International, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT ROBERT ELMAN's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Plaintiff Unionn LLC's Person(S) Most Qualified, Defendant Nina Hatvany And Defendant Pacific Union International, Inc.'S Person(S) Most Qualified, And For Sanctions. Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Counsel should bring their calendars. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS MEYERS VS. ATHENA ZEIGLER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 13, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT ATHENA ZEIGLER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Based on review of the papers filed by the parties, this civil case is stayed per CCP 128(a)(8) pending the conclusion of all proceedings in the family court relating to the claims alleged by plaintiff Nicholas Meyers. Mr. Meyers' claims regarding the 401K account were plainly part of the family court action (FDI-17-787579). (Marital Settlement Agreement ("MSA"),VI.B(b); Qualified Domestic Relations Order, 2:11; 2:20-21.) Mr. Meyers must return to family court for clarification of any issues pertaining to the 401K account. The family court expressly reserved its jurisdiction to resolve such matters per section X of the MSA. This civil court cannot undermine the family court's prior adjudications. "After a family law court acquires jurisdiction to divide community property in a dissolution action, no other department of a superior court may make an order adversely affecting that division." (Askew v. Askew (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 942, 961; see also Burkle v. Burkle (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 387, 393.) The record is not clear whether the family court made any explicit or implicit rulings with regard to the parties' dog Luna. Since California considers a dog personal property and the family court resolved the parties' personal property issues, this court believes that the family court should resolve and/or clarify that issue too. (Civil Code § 3340; Plotnik v. Meihaus (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1590, 1606.) In an abundance of caution, this court will stay this action rather than dismiss it outright to allow Mr. Meyers the opportunity to return to the family court for guidance. If Mr. Meyers fails to seek relief with the family court by February 28, 2019, defendant Athena Ziegler may apply to this court ex parte for a dismissal of this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Ziegler is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA MOORE ET AL VS. MEGAN ROHRER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Response To Plaintiffs First Set Of Document Requests
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs BRENDA MOORE, ANDREA MURPHY, TRAVIS O'HARA, BERNICE LASSITER, ADAH BUIKE, and JEANNIE WILSON's Motion To Compel Defendants Response To Plaintiffs First Set Of Document Requests. Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion to compel defendants' responses to plaintiffs' first set of document requests is granted. Defendants failed to timely serve responses or objections and have provided no explanation or excuse for raising objections for the first time in opposition papers. Defendants have waived all objections and shall provide Code-compliant verified responses, without objections, and produce all responsive documents by December 27, 2018. Defendants may provide party-specific verifications should these be necessary. Defendants shall pay sanctions in the amount of $5,610 by December 27, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
